scope.summary: Plan §30.4 item 11, both halves: the OAuth reject reasons are now bound to the TokenError enum by an exhaustive match plus a single table, and the verifier tamper matrix covers every payload leaf at every depth instead of one field.
out_of_scope: The ten pre-existing per-reason OAuth tests, which are kept: they carry ordering assertions (type rejected before claim validation) the table does not, and AGENTS.md RULE 1 forbids deleting them.
crates/oraclemcp-auth/src/http_guard.rs, another agent's mid-edit throughout. TokenError::Missing is produced by extract_bearer in oauth_rs.rs, so the matrix covers it without touching that file.
Signature-scheme coverage beyond HS256: the asymmetric verifier is a fail-closed seam with no implementation, so there is nothing to tamper-test there yet.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp-auth (oauth_reject_matrix_covers_every_reason_and_the_accept)
entry_points.trace: 58 tests pass. The audit the item asked for: all ten reject reasons already had individual tests, so the gap was not coverage but the absence of any link between the #[non_exhaustive] enum and the test list. all_token_errors() carries an in-crate exhaustive match (a new variant stops the crate compiling), and the table requires a row that provokes each reason through the real validator, compared by std::mem::discriminant so UnsupportedAlg/UntrustedIssuer count only when actually produced. MUTATION-CHECKED: deleting the nbf row fails with 'no row in the reject matrix provokes NotYetValid'.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp-verifier --test test_attestation (every_payload_leaf_at_every_depth_is_covered_by_the_signature)
entry_points.trace: 11 tests pass. The matrix walks every scalar leaf of the signed payload (>=12 leaves, at least one at depth >=3) and asserts each tamper is rejected, then asserts separately that a SCHEMA-VALID tamper at depth 3 is caught by the payload digest specifically. Writing it surfaced that /artifacts/0/sha256 is rejected by InvalidArtifactDigest before the digest check runs — the field is covered, but by an earlier gate, which is why the specificity rows use schema-valid replacements. MUTATION-CHECKED: inverting the totality assertion fails on /artifacts/0/path.

limitations: The tamper matrix's control assertion (an untampered re-serialization still verifies) is what keeps it honest; if the payload encoding ever stops round-tripping byte-identically, that control fails first and the matrix must be rewritten to tamper raw bytes rather than re-serialized JSON.
Only the payload line is walked. The signature line's own fields are covered by the existing wrong-key, unknown-key-id, and recomputed-digest-forgery tests, not by this matrix.
Scoped verification: cargo test/clippy for -p oraclemcp-auth and -p oraclemcp-verifier. No workspace-wide run; rustfmt applied to the two touched files only.
